_meta.note: POC3 baked-in test dataset. Single source of truth for the storefront preview. NOT wired to live Shopify products. When the production store is built, this is replaced by real {{ product }} / {{ collection }} objects + crema_italia.* metafields. See CLAUDE.md.

roasters.name: Gardelli Specialty Coffee
roasters.label: Gardelli
roasters.blurb: World-championship palates, exacting light roasts, named farms.
bio: Rubens Gardelli built his reputation on competition tables before he built a roastery. The house style is light, clean, and unmistakably origin-forward - coffees chosen for what the farm and the process can say on their own.
We import a small selection of Gardelli's bags. They reach you exactly as Gardelli sealed them: whole bean, in valve bags, nothing repackaged.

roasters.name: Torrefazione La Sosta
roasters.label: La Sosta
roasters.blurb: Classic Florentine balance - caramel, nuts, gentle sweetness.
bio: A Florentine roastery in the classic mold - the cup the city actually drinks. La Sosta roasts for balance and sweetness, the everyday espresso that rewards a good machine and a steady hand.
We import their Guatemala year-round and a seasonal Christmas edition. Whole bean, valve-sealed, unchanged.

roasters.name: Caffè Fusari
roasters.label: Fusari
roasters.blurb: Dark, sweet, and chocolatey - espresso as Nonna makes it.
bio: Caffè Fusari roasts the Tuscan bar cup: dark, round, and sweet, built for the moka pot and the espresso machine. A family roastery that has changed very little, on purpose.
We import their Colombia year-round and a monsooned Indian lot for the winter. Whole bean, valve-sealed, unchanged.

roasters.name: Garage Coffee Bros
roasters.label: GCB
bio: Two brothers who started in a garage in Prato and never lost the experimental streak. Garage Coffee Bros chase character - the cup that makes you stop and ask what it is.
We import a rotating single-origin and a Brazilian for the subscription shelf. Whole bean, valve-sealed, unchanged.

roasters.name: Torrefazione Val d'Orcia
roasters.label: Val d'Orcia
roasters.blurb: The decaf that started Crema Italia - walnut, dried fig, cocoa.
bio: A small Sienese roastery in the Val d'Orcia, as much a part of the landscape as the cypress rows. Their decaf is the cup that started Crema Italia - proof that decaffeinated need not mean diminished.
We import their sugarcane-decaf year-round and a house blend. Whole bean, valve-sealed, unchanged.

products.title: Ethiopia Bombe
products.long: A natural-process Yirgacheffe roasted light to keep its perfume intact - jasmine and bergamot up top, ripe peach underneath. Imported whole bean in Gardelli's valve-sealed bag.
products.brewing: Excellent as filter or in a moka pot. We sell whole bean only - grind fresh just before you brew.

products.title: Rwanda Nyamasheke
products.blurb: Red currant, black tea, lime. Washed.
products.long: A washed Rwandan from the hills of Nyamasheke, roasted light and clean. Sparkling acidity, red-fruit clarity, a black-tea finish.
products.brewing: Best as pour-over or filter. Whole bean only - grind fresh just before you brew.

products.title: Guatemala Huehuetenango
products.long: A washed Huehuetenango roasted to a classic Florentine medium - brown sugar and almond, with a dried-apricot sweetness that suits a moka pot or an espresso machine.
products.brewing: Built for moka and espresso. Whole bean only - grind fresh just before you brew.

products.title: Brasile Cerrado
products.long: A natural Cerrado for the everyday cup - roasted, sweet, and low in acidity. The dependable house espresso.
products.brewing: Ideal for espresso and moka. Whole bean only - grind fresh just before you brew.

products.title: Colombia Antioquia
products.long: A washed Antioquia roasted dark in the Tuscan bar tradition - milk chocolate and hazelnut, a caramel sweetness, almost no edge. Nonna's espresso.
products.brewing: Made for espresso and moka. Whole bean only - grind fresh just before you brew.

products.title: India Monsooned Malabar
products.blurb: Dark chocolate, pipe tobacco, baking spice.
products.long: A monsooned Malabar - low acidity, full body, a savoury depth of tobacco and spice. A winter espresso with character.
products.brewing: Espresso and moka. Whole bean only - grind fresh just before you brew.

products.title: Brasile Daterra
products.long: A pulped-natural Daterra roasted medium - clean, sweet, and comforting. The brothers' take on a crowd-pleasing everyday bean.
products.brewing: Espresso, moka, or filter. Whole bean only - grind fresh just before you brew.

products.title: Val d'Orcia Decaffeinato
products.blurb: Walnut, dried fig, cocoa. The cup that started Crema Italia.
products.long: A sugarcane-process decaf from Colombia, roasted medium - walnut, dried fig, and cocoa, with none of the flatness decaf is unfairly known for. The cup that started Crema Italia.
products.brewing: Lovely as an evening espresso or moka. Whole bean only - grind fresh just before you brew.

products.title: Miscela Classica
products.blurb: Cocoa, toasted almond, caramel. A signature blend.
products.long: The roastery's signature blend - Brazilian body, a touch of Ethiopian lift, Indian depth. Cocoa and toasted almond, built for the bar cup.
products.brewing: Espresso and moka. Whole bean only - grind fresh just before you brew.

products.title: Tour d'Italia 1
products.blurb: Three 100g bags, three roasters, assembled on-demand. Printed tasting card included.
products.long: A journey across regions: North, Central, South. Three 100g bags - Gardelli Ethiopia, La Sosta Guatemala, Fusari Colombia - boxed with a tasting card and assembled to order so every bag ships fresh.
products.brewing: Each bag is whole bean - grind fresh just before you brew. The card suggests a method for each.

products.title: Finca Deborah Geisha
products.blurb: Chamomile, white peach, jasmine. Panama micro-lot.
products.long: A washed Geisha from Finca Deborah, high on the Volcán slopes - chamomile, white peach, jasmine. A micro-lot we secured a few kilos of; when this pallet is gone it may not return.
products.brewing: Reserve it for pour-over, where the florals carry. Whole bean only - grind fresh just before you brew.

products.title: Edizione di Natale
products.blurb: Panettone, candied orange, hazelnut. Holiday micro-lot.
products.long: La Sosta's holiday blend - warm, sweet, and spiced, the cup that tastes like panettone on the table. A seasonal Selezione micro-lot.
products.brewing: Espresso or moka, alongside something sweet. Whole bean only - grind fresh just before you brew.

products.title: La Dolce Vita
products.display_title: Garage Coffee Bros - La Dolce Vita
products.long: A dark house blend that reached its Offerta threshold - still good coffee, still inside a real freshness window, priced down honestly. Sold as-is.
products.brewing: Espresso and moka. Whole bean only - grind fresh just before you brew.

products.title: Bialetti Moka Express - 3 cup
products.blurb: The classic stovetop. Aluminium, made in Italy.

products.title: Hand Burr Grinder
products.blurb: Grind fresh, just before you brew. Stainless burrs.

products.title: Espresso Cups - pair
products.blurb: Porcelain, thick wall, Crema Italia mark.

people.name: Lucia Calò
bio: My connection to the world of hospitality runs deep, starting within my own family of restaurateurs. When my parents opened a café, my summer school breaks became a hands-on introduction to the art of coffee, where I proudly brewed my very first espressos.

At 28, my journey led me to Australia, where I lived for four unforgettable years. It was during this time that my passion for coffee truly came full circle: working as a barista in a country where coffee culture is taken to an elite level - and where the craft is highly respected - not only connected me to the local scene but beautifully funded my studies.

This experience allowed me to later transition back to my chosen path as a professional massage therapist. Fueled by a lifelong love for high-quality coffee, I still turn every trip around the globe into a quest for the perfect cup - always searching for excellence, one bean at a time.

people.name: Asia Chirdo
bio: Silvia Asia Chirdo is a restaurant and hospitality consultant with 25 years of experience across the Italian food and HORECA industry. Since 2008, she has advised restaurants, food retail concepts, and hospitality brands in Italy and across Europe, with projects in Rome, Milan, and beyond. Her core strength lies in brand creation, launch, and development - building concepts from the ground up, defining identity and positioning, and guiding them through startup into sustainable growth.
